root_text.an3.55:1.1: Atha kho jāṇussoṇi brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho jāṇussoṇi brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.55:1.2: “‘sandiṭṭhikaṁ nibbānaṁ sandiṭṭhikaṁ nibbānan’ti, bho gotama, vuccati.
root_text.an3.55:1.3: Kittāvatā nu kho, bho gotama, sandiṭṭhikaṁ nibbānaṁ hoti akālikaṁ ehipassikaṁ opaneyyikaṁ paccattaṁ veditabbaṁ viññūhī”ti?
root_text.an3.55:2.1: Ratto kho, brāhmaṇa, rāgena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto attabyābādhāyapi ceteti, parabyābādhāyapi ceteti, ubhayabyābādhāyapi ceteti, cetasikampi dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti.
root_text.an3.55:2.2: Rāge pahīne nevattabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na parabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na ubhayabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na cetasikampi dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti.
root_text.an3.55:2.3: Evampi kho, brāhmaṇa, sandiṭṭhikaṁ nibbānaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.55:3.2: mūḷho kho, brāhmaṇa, mohena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto attabyābādhāyapi ceteti, parabyābādhāyapi ceteti, ubhayabyābādhāyapi ceteti, cetasikampi dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti.
root_text.an3.55:3.3: Mohe pahīne nevattabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na parabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na ubhayabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na cetasikaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti.
root_text.an3.55:3.4: Evampi kho, brāhmaṇa, sandiṭṭhikaṁ nibbānaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.55:4.1: Yato kho ayaṁ, brāhmaṇa, anavasesaṁ rāgakkhayaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti, anavasesaṁ dosakkhayaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti, anavasesaṁ mohakkhayaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti;
root_text.an3.55:4.2: evaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, sandiṭṭhikaṁ nibbānaṁ hoti akālikaṁ ehipassikaṁ opaneyyikaṁ paccattaṁ veditabbaṁ viññūhī”ti.
root_text.an3.55:5.2: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.an3.55:1.1: Then the brahmin Jānussoṇi went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an3.55:1.2: “Worthy Gotama, they say that ‘extinguishment is apparent in the present life’.
translation_text.an3.55:1.3: In what way is extinguishment apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves?”
translation_text.an3.55:2.1: “A greedy person, overcome by greed, intends to hurt themselves, hurt others, and hurt both. They experience mental pain and sadness.
translation_text.an3.55:2.2: When greed has been given up, they don’t intend to hurt themselves, hurt others, and hurt both. They don’t experience mental pain and sadness.
translation_text.an3.55:2.3: This is how extinguishment is apparent in the present life.
translation_text.an3.55:3.2: A deluded person, overcome by delusion, intends to hurt themselves, hurt others, and hurt both. They experience mental pain and sadness.
translation_text.an3.55:3.3: When delusion has been given up, they don’t intend to hurt themselves, hurt others, and hurt both. They don’t experience mental pain and sadness.
translation_text.an3.55:3.4: This, too, is how extinguishment is apparent in the present life.
translation_text.an3.55:4.1: When you experience the ending of greed, hate, and delusion without anything left over,
translation_text.an3.55:4.2: that’s how extinguishment is apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves.”
translation_text.an3.55:5.2: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

variant_text.an3.55:4.1: Yato kho ayaṁ, brāhmaṇa → yato ca kho ayaṁ brāhmaṇa (bj); yato kho brāhmaṇa akālikaṁ ehipassikaṁ opaneyyikaṁ paccattaṁ veditabbaṁ (mr)
